steps.caption_md: Pointers start at the two ends of `"Do geese see God?"`. `hi` sits on `?`, which isn't alphanumeric, so it slides inward before any comparison happens.
`hi` now sits on `d`. Comparing case-insensitively, `D` == `d` — a match. Both pointers step inward.
`lo` holds `o`, `hi` holds `o` — another match. The pointers keep converging one pair at a time.

predict.prompt_md: `lo` and `hi` just matched again. What does the algorithm do next?

choices.label_md: Step both pointers one position further inward and compare the next pair.
Go back to index 0 to double-check the earlier matches.
Build a reversed copy of the remaining substring to verify the rest at once.

predict.explanation_md: There's no need to recheck or build a copy — the pointers simply keep converging, one pair per step, skipping non-alphanumeric characters as they go.

steps.caption_md: The pointers have converged to the middle without ever disagreeing. `lo` is no longer less than `hi`, so the loop ends.
Every pair matched before the pointers crossed — the cleaned sequence reads the same from both ends, so the answer is `True`.
